<!-- managed by: auto-docs · do not edit by hand · see .agents/auto-docs.md -->

# Maintainer docs

For people **contributing to** this project. The shape of the code, the seams,
the conventions — what you'd need before opening a PR or designing a new
feature.

This folder is regenerated by the **auto-docs** scheduled agent — see
[`.agents/auto-docs.md`](../../.agents/auto-docs.md). Files here are
agent-owned: amend the source (code + agent spec) rather than the rendered
docs.

## What lives here

- **`architecture.md`** — layered overview (modules, data flow, key seams).
- **`api-reference.md`** — exported types + functions grouped by module.
- **`ipc-reference.md`** — every IPC channel: name, params, returns.
- **`module-catalog.md`** — one-line summary per top-level source file.
- **`tab-catalog.md`** — TerMinal-style tab/plugin/widget catalog (only when
  the relevant patterns are detected in the repo).

## When this regenerates

The auto-docs agent re-runs on a schedule (default weekly). It scans
`src/**/*.{ts,tsx,py,go,rs}`, IPC patterns, and module-level doc comments. If
nothing material changed since the last `lastScannedSha`, the run is a no-op.

## Human-authored docs

Hand-written maintainer content (handbook chapters, design notes, anything
agent-resistant) belongs **outside** this folder — e.g., `docs/handbook/`,
`docs/architecture.md` at the docs root, or `docs/runbooks/`.
